title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
logline: After a painful breakup, a man undergoes a procedure to erase his ex-girlfriend from his memory, only to realize mid-erasure that he wants to keep her.
antagonistOrConflict: The erasure procedure itself and Joel's own emotional avoidance; the conflict between memory and forgetting
tone: Melancholic, surreal, bittersweet, emotionally raw with moments of whimsy

actBreaks.description: The erasure procedure begins in Joel's apartment, shifting from the 'present day' meeting with Clementine to the reverse-chronological memory deletion

validation.ruling: Scene #50 shows the procedure already underway, not the act break itself. Scene #47 (p39, 30%) is the true Act 1 break: Mierzwiak explains the erasure process to Joel in the examination chair, the room colors fade, and his voice becomes dry and monotonous—this is the irreversible moment Joel enters the procedure, fundamentally changing the story from external conflict to internal mindscape journey.

actBreaks.description: Joel's resistance to the erasure reaches crisis as he tries to hide Clementine in non-Clementine memories, fundamentally changing his relationship to the procedure

validation.ruling: Scene #111 (p81, 62.3%) is the Act 2 break: Joel and Clementine run toward their beach house but it's gone—the scene is decaying, Joel grabs her arm and yanks, marking the irreversible collapse of their shared memories and forcing the story into its final act where only childhood memories remain.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a reverse-chronological memory spiral wrapped inside a forward-moving frame story. The opening 11 scenes establish what feels like a meet-cute between Joel and Clementine on a train to Montauk, but scene 16 onward reveals this is actually a reunion after mutual memory erasure. The script then plunges backward through Joel's memories during his own erasure procedure (beginning around scene 50), moving from the bitter end of their relationship toward its hopeful beginning. This creates a devastating emotional architecture: we experience the relationship's decay before understanding its beauty, making the final beach house memory (scenes 140-141) land with enormous weight. The non-linear structure isn't a gimmick—it's the story's emotional engine, forcing us to grieve the relationship in reverse and understand why Joel fights to keep what he initially wanted to forget.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing operates in three distinct registers. Scenes 1-49 move with deliberate mystery, withholding information while establishing the Lacuna procedure. Scene 50 onward accelerates into a frantic, surreal chase through deteriorating memories, with very short scenes (often under a page) creating mounting desperation. The final act (scenes 145-171) returns to measured, real-time pacing as characters confront the truth. The script's 1.2-page average scene length masks wild variation—some memories are single-beat fragments while others (like the beach house sequence, scenes 140-141) luxuriate in extended dialogue.

incitingIncident.description: Rob pulls a yellow Lacuna card from a drawer and hands it to Joel, who reads that Clementine has had him erased from her memory.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: 33 INT. ROB AND CARRIE'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 33

validation.ruling: Scene #32 is merely Joel buying a gift, not the inciting incident. Scene #34 (p31, 24%) is the true inciting incident: Rob hands Joel the Lacuna card revealing Clementine has erased him from her memory—this shatters Joel's ordinary world, establishes the irreversible stakes, and forces him to take action by going to Lacuna himself.

midpoint.description: Joel sits across from a faded Mierzwiak and desperately begs him to stop erasing Clementine because he loves her, but Mierzwiak says he's just something Joel is imagining.

validation.ruling: Scene #82 (p63, 48.5%) is the midpoint where Joel shifts from reactive to proactive: he confronts Mierzwiak inside his own mind, desperately pleading to stop the erasure because he loves Clementine, then runs from the office to pursue Patrick—this is the point of no return where Joel actively fights the procedure rather than passively experiencing it.

climax.description: Joel enters the darkened beach house and Clementine closes the door behind him; she finds a flashlight, searches for candles and alcohol, declares it their house for the night as David and Ruth Laskin, and tells Joel to make drinks while she goes to slip into something more Ruth.

validation.ruling: Scene #141 is setup/courtship, not the climax. Scene #142 (p105, 81%) is the emotional climax: Joel enters the beach house with Clementine, she declares it their house as David and Ruth Laskin, and they create their most intimate memory together—this is the peak emotional confrontation where Joel must face losing this defining moment, demonstrating his character arc completion as he fully commits to the relationship.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT. COMMUTER TRAIN STATION - DAY 1
scenes.summary: A suited man breaks from a crowded commuter platform, rushes across an overpass, and boards an almost empty train heading in the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. MONTAUK TRAIN STATION - DAY 2
scenes.summary: Joel calls in sick to work from a payphone at Montauk train station, claiming food poisoning while the wind howls around him.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. BEACH - DAY 3
scenes.summary: Joel wanders the empty, windy beach with his briefcase and nods at an old man with a metal detector.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. BEACH - DAY 4
scenes.summary: Joel looks out at the ocean.

scenes.heading: 5 EXT. BEACH - DAY 5
scenes.summary: Joel reads his last journal entry about coexisting with Naomi, discovers many pages have been torn out, and writes a new entry about being on a train to Montauk.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. BEACH - DAY 6
scenes.summary: Joel peeks cautiously into the dark window of a closed beach house.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. BEACH - DAY 7
scenes.summary: Joel digs into the sand with a stick.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. DINER - DAY 8
scenes.summary: Joel eats alone in an off-season diner, drawing in his notebook, when Clementine enters with bright blue hair and sits in another booth.

scenes.heading: 9 EXT. BEACH - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine both stare at the ocean from different positions on the beach, and Joel glances sideways at her.

scenes.heading: 10 EXT. MONTAUK TRAIN STATION PLATFORM - LATE AFTERNOON 10
scenes.summary: Clementine waves enthusiastically at Joel on the empty train platform, then sits far away while Joel writes in his journal about falling in love with women who show him attention.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. TRAIN - LATE AFTERNOON 11
scenes.summary: Clementine approaches Joel on the train, they have an awkward but playful conversation, she reveals her hair color changes and her name, and they discuss Montauk, Pope Alexander, and Joel's quiet nature.
scenes.turningPointNote: First substantial interaction between Joel and Clementine, establishing their connection and dynamic

scenes.heading: 12 INT. TRAIN - DUSK 12
scenes.summary: Clementine watches Joel as he draws her in his notebook on the more crowded train.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. TRAIN - NIGHT 13
scenes.summary: On the crowded night train, Joel stares out the window while Clementine sits closer and eyes him.

scenes.heading: 14 EXT. TRAIN STATION - NIGHT 14
scenes.summary: Joel exits the train and discovers a big dented scrape along his car in the parking lot.

scenes.heading: 15 INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 15
scenes.summary: Joel offers Clementine a ride, she accepts after joking about stalkers, and he drives her to Sherman Drive where she invites him in for a drink.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 16
scenes.summary: Joel nervously stands in Clementine's apartment examining her belongings while she prepares drinks, they discuss a crow photograph, and she invites him to lie on the ice with her on the frozen Charles River.

scenes.heading: 17 INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 17
scenes.summary: Joel drives home agitated, parks behind his apartment building, and heads around to the front.

scenes.heading: 17A INT. VAN - NIGHT 17A
scenes.summary: Stan and Patrick in a van spot Joel, follow him slowly, and park across the street from his building.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 18 *
scenes.summary: Joel looks back at the van, sees a hand wave cheerily from inside, and enters his building where Frank mentions Joel is lucky to have Clementine.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT ENTRANCEWAY - CONTINUOUS 19 *
scenes.summary: Joel changes into fresh pajamas, swallows a pink pill from a vial, looks around panicked as if going through a checklist, and tries to see into the van across the street from his window.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 20 *
scenes.summary: Stan and Patrick watch Joel from the van as he looks out at them, then the lights in Joel's apartment click off and they prepare for the procedure.

scenes.heading: 21 INT./EXT. VAN - CONTINUOUS 21 *
scenes.summary: Stan and Patrick pull machines from the van and head up Joel's apartment building walkway.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. VAN - CONTINUOUS 22 *
scenes.summary: Stan unlocks Joel's apartment door with a key, he and Patrick enter and switch on the light as Patrick hums unconsciously.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - MOMENTS LATER 23 *
scenes.summary: In a vague-looking room, Joel changes into pajamas, examines a pill with an unreadable code, swallows it, looks around panicked, and tries to see into the van outside while a voice-over asks if everything is ready.
scenes.turningPointNote: The erasure procedure begins as Joel's memory becomes unreliable and fragmented

scenes.heading: 24 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 24
scenes.summary: Joel spots the van with two figures, a hand waves and there's laughter, and he hurries inside the building with loud footsteps.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT 25
scenes.summary: Frank asks Joel about Valentine's plans and mentions Joel is lucky to have Clementine, while Joel stares at a yellow Lacuna envelope in Frank's mail and sees blue dots on his own reflection.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT ENTRANCEWAY - NIGHT 26
scenes.summary: Joel paces at Rob and Carrie's house clutching a wrapped gift, explaining he called Clementine on Valentine's Day to resolve their issues.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. ROB AND CARRIE'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 27
scenes.summary: Joel dials Clementine's number and hears a recording that the number has been disconnected.

scenes.heading: 28 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 28
scenes.summary: Joel examines funky necklaces in Antic Attic while explaining he hurried there to find something for Clementine.

scenes.heading: 29 INT. STORE (ANTIC ATTIC) - NIGHT 29
scenes.summary: Exterior shot of Antic Attic.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. ANTIC ATTIC - NIGHT 30
scenes.summary: A saleswoman wraps jewelry in red paper while Joel explains he wanted to give Clementine an early Valentine, and a hand writes an apology on a heart-shaped card.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. STORE (ANTIC ATTIC) - NIGHT 31
scenes.summary: Joel approaches Clementine at Barnes and Noble with the wrapped gift, but she treats him like a stranger with a professional smile while Patrick approaches her from behind.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel discovers Clementine doesn't recognize him, the devastating revelation that drives him to Lacuna

scenes.heading: 32 INT. BARNES AND NOBLE BOOKSTORE - NIGHT 32
scenes.summary: Joel asks Rob and Carrie why Clementine would treat him like a stranger, they debate whether he should go to her house, and Rob reveals he has something to show Joel.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. ROB AND CARRIE'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 33
scenes.summary: Rob pulls a yellow Lacuna card from a drawer and hands it to Joel, who reads that Clementine has had him erased from her memory.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel learns Clementine has erased him, the central inciting incident

scenes.heading: 33C INT. ROB AND CARRIE'S KITCHEN - NIGHT 33C
scenes.summary: Joel walks along the street, sees a flash of himself ahead almost hit by a truck, and pushes open the door to Lacuna Inc.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. LACUNA - DAY 34
scenes.summary: Joel waits at the Lacuna reception desk while Mary busily answers phones and prints envelopes, then tells her he has an appointment with Dr. Mierzwiak.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. LACUNA WAITING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 35 *
scenes.summary: Joel walks behind Mary down the Lacuna hallway, Stan pops out to say hi to Mary, and Mary shows Joel to Mierzwiak's office.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. LACUNA OFFICE HALL - MOMENTS LATER 36 *
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak confirms the yellow card is real and that Clementine was unhappy and wanted to move on, while Mary watches Mierzwiak longingly from behind.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. LACUNA, HOWARD'S OFFICE - DAY 37
scenes.summary: Carrie explains that Clementine met someone at the supermarket who told her about Lacuna, while Rob hammers a birdhouse in the other room.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. ROB AND CARRIE'S KITCHEN - DAY / INT. LACUNA - DAY 38
scenes.summary: Joel sits in his car crying outside a drive-in theater as the windows fog up until the exterior is obliterated.

scenes.heading: 39 INT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 39
scenes.summary: Joel barges into Mierzwiak's office demanding the procedure be done now, and Mierzwiak agrees to accommodate him despite the pre-Valentine's Day rush.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel commits to erasing Clementine, setting the procedure in motion

scenes.heading: 40 INT. LACUNA, MIERZWIAK'S OFFICE - NIGHT 40
scenes.summary: Joel collects objects associated with Clementine into garbage bags, including books, toiletries, clothing, photographs, and a photo of her as a child in a pink cowboy hat.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - DAY 41
scenes.summary: Joel walks carrying two garbage bags and is almost hit by a truck as he crosses the street, replaying the near-accident he witnessed earlier.

scenes.heading: 42 EXT. NEW YORK STREET - DAY 42
scenes.summary: Joel sits in the Lacuna waiting room with his garbage bags while a woman with dog paraphernalia sits across from him, and Mierzwiak calls him in.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. LACUNA WAITING ROOM - DAY 43
scenes.summary: Joel and Mierzwiak walk past Mary in the hallway, Mierzwiak introduces Stan who will handle Joel's case, and Stan shakes Joel's hand.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. LACUNA HALLWAY - DAY 44
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak records Joel stating his name and that he's there to erase Clementine Kruczynski, then Joel begins recounting how he met her at a beach party while living with Naomi.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. MIERZWIAK'S OFFICE - DAY 45
scenes.summary: Stan draws blue dots on Joel's forehead in the examination chair while Mierzwiak explains the erasure process, and the room colors start to fade as Mierzwiak's voice becomes dry and monotonous.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mapping procedure begins, Joel enters his own memories

scenes.heading: 46A INT. LAB - DAY 46A
scenes.summary: Joel watches himself in the chair from outside his body, experiences deja vu, realizes he's already in his head, and Stan pulls a snow globe from one of Joel's garbage bags.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. LACUNA LAB - DAY 47
scenes.summary: Joel lies on his back with electrodes connecting his head to machines operated by Stan and Patrick, who monitor the voltage and brain activity.
scenes.turningPointNote: The actual erasure procedure begins in Joel's apartment

scenes.heading: 48 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 48
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 49 INT. LACUNA LAB ROOM - DAY 49
scenes.summary: Stan reads aloud from Joel's journal about meeting Clementine while the memory degrades around them, and Joel searches for the disembodied voices.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 50
scenes.summary: Patrick evaluates Joel's apartment while Stan monitors equipment, and Patrick asks Stan who is better-looking between him and the unconscious Joel.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 51
scenes.summary: Joel sits in his dark, vague room listening to Stan's voice saying Mary is coming over.

scenes.heading: 52 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 52
scenes.summary: Stan works the joystick while Patrick tells him about his new girlfriend, and Stan dismisses the conversation to focus on erasing a memory.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 53
scenes.summary: Clementine staggers in drunk at 3am saying she wrecked Joel's car, Joel calls her pathetic and irresponsible, and the scene begins to degrade.

scenes.heading: 53A INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT 53A
scenes.summary: Joel looks for Clementine in the hallway but she is gone.

scenes.heading: 54 EXT. JOEL'S STREET - NIGHT 54
scenes.summary: Joel looks at his dented car slammed against a fire hydrant and spots Clementine walking away in the distance.

scenes.heading: 55 INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 55
scenes.summary: Joel drives after Clementine and yells that he's erasing her and is happy, then gets out of the car screaming that she did it to him first.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. STREET - NIGHT 56
scenes.summary: Joel yells after Clementine on a dreamlike degrading street where she walks in a loop, never getting farther away despite his running after her.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 57
scenes.summary: Patrick confesses to Stan that he fell in love with the unconscious girl they erased and stole her panties.

scenes.heading: 58 EXT. STREET - NIGHT 58
scenes.summary: Joel listens to Patrick and Stan's conversation while the scene of him and Clementine eating dinner fades away around them.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 59
scenes.summary: Patrick's voice reveals he asked out the erased girl after the procedure, Joel reacts in shock, and Stan calls it unethical.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 60
scenes.summary: Joel pretends to be dead on the floor but Clementine ignores him, complains about the TV, and leaves saying she should have left him at the flea market.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. FLEA MARKET - DAY 61
scenes.summary: At a flea market, Clementine says she wants a baby, Joel says they're not ready, and she violently turns on him asking what he means and calling him a ventriloquist.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. BAR - NIGHT 63
scenes.summary: Joel brings drinks to Clementine who is sitting with Mark, she tells Joel that Mark likes her boobs and doesn't think she's fat, and the scene fades as Mark leaves.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 64
scenes.summary: Mary arrives at Joel's apartment, Patrick confesses to Stan that he's been using Joel's words and actions with his new girlfriend who is the erased patient Clementine.
scenes.turningPointNote: Patrick reveals he is dating Clementine and using Joel's memories to seduce her, creating a major ethical violation and emotional betrayal

scenes.heading: 65 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 65
scenes.summary: In a degrading memory, Joel and Clementine lie in bed where she robotically complains he doesn't share while he quotes a film line about silence and communication.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. CHINESE RESTAURANT - NIGHT 66
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine eat dinner in silence at a Chinese restaurant while Joel observes other couples and worries internally about being bored with each other.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. BARNES AND NOBLE BOOKSTORE - NIGHT 67
scenes.summary: Joel watches a decayed memory of Patrick approaching Clementine at Barnes and Noble, trying unsuccessfully to see Patrick's shadowed face clearly.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. CHINESE RESTAURANT - NIGHT 68
scenes.summary: Back in the Chinese restaurant memory, Joel listens to Patrick's voice on the phone.

scenes.heading: 69 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 69
scenes.summary: Patrick asks Stan if he can leave to see his upset girlfriend nearby, Mary tells Stan to let him go, and Patrick quietly thanks Mary before leaving.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. VOID - DAY 70
scenes.summary: Clementine materializes in an orange sweatshirt matching her orange hair, Joel asks how Patrick knew to call her tangerine, and something begins to click for Joel.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 71
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine lie under a blanket listening to music while she cries reading from The Velveteen Rabbit about what it means to be real.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 72
scenes.summary: Stan and Mary smoke a joint and watch the monitor, Mary praises Howard's gift of letting people begin again, and Stan asks if she loves Howard.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. CLEMENTINE'S STREET - NIGHT 73
scenes.summary: Patrick trudges down the block bundled up and carrying a full backpack.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 74
scenes.summary: Clementine watches Patrick arrive and hugs him crying that she's disappearing, asks him to come to Boston tonight, and he agrees.

scenes.heading: 75 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 75 *
scenes.summary: Stan tells someone on the phone he can handle the procedure on auto-pilot while watching stoned Mary dance.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - DAY 76 *
scenes.summary: Patrick rifles through his backpack, pulls out Joel's Valentine's gift and letters, then reads Clementine's letter thanking Joel for the Charles River night.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. CHARLES RIVER - NIGHT 77
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine lie on the frozen Charles River looking at stars, Joel says he's happy and exactly where he wants to be, then panics as the scene erases and screams to call it off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel realizes he wants to keep his memories of Clementine and desperately tries to stop the erasure procedure

scenes.heading: 78 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS 78 *
scenes.summary: Joel's eyes roll almost imperceptibly while Stan and Mary dance together, not watching him.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. CHARLES RIVER - CONTINUOUS 79 *
scenes.summary: Joel grabs the fading Clementine's hand and runs toward shore through a montage of decaying memories while Stan and Mary's dance music drifts through.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. LACUNA MIERZWIAK'S OFFICE - NIGHT 82
scenes.summary: Joel sits across from a faded Mierzwiak and desperately begs him to stop erasing Clementine because he loves her, but Mierzwiak says he's just something Joel is imagining.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel directly confronts the architect of the erasure and pleads to stop the procedure, but realizes he cannot communicate with the real world

scenes.heading: 82A INT. HALL - NIGHT 82A
scenes.summary: Joel runs toward the shadowy Patrick in a hallway but doesn't get any closer.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 83
scenes.summary: Patrick reads Clementine's letter about making love on the ice, then gives her Joel's Valentine's gift claiming it's from him, and she pulls out the necklace saying it's beautiful.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 84
scenes.summary: Stan and Mary have sex on the floor next to Joel's bed.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT. FOREST - DAY 85
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine hike through a degrading forest while Joel panics that they're erasing her and Clementine tells him to calm down and enjoy the scenery.

scenes.heading: 85A INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 85A
scenes.summary: Joel briefly sees through his own eyes at the apartment ceiling with vague sounds of sex in the background.

scenes.heading: 85B EXT. FOREST - DAY 85B
scenes.summary: Joel tells Clementine he briefly woke but couldn't move or do anything, and they argue about whether his attempt to signal the real world worked.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - DAY 86
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine huddle on the couch reading together in the rain, Joel cherishes the memory of her skin and sexiness, and Clementine proposes hiding in memories from before they met.
scenes.turningPointNote: Clementine suggests the plan to hide in Joel's childhood memories to preserve their relationship

scenes.heading: 88 INT. DATED KITCHEN - DAY 88
scenes.summary: Four-year-old Joel hides under the kitchen table while his mother talks to a neighbor with Clementine's face, then Clementine bends down and says it worked.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 89
scenes.summary: Stan notices the erasure has stopped and Joel has disappeared from the screen, he panics not knowing what to do, and Mary tells him to call Howard.
scenes.turningPointNote: The erasure procedure malfunctions when Joel hides in childhood memories, forcing Stan to call Mierzwiak for help

scenes.heading: 90 INT. MIERZWIAK'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 90
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak groggily answers Stan's call in bed while his wife listens, Stan explains Joel disappeared from the map, and Mierzwiak asks for the address.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 91
scenes.summary: Stan hangs up and tells Mary to leave, but she refuses and rushes to the bathroom stoned to fix her appearance before Mierzwiak arrives.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. KITCHEN - DAY 92
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine have sex under the kitchen table while Joel's mother pats his head, and Clementine tells Joel he'll remember her in the morning.

scenes.heading: 93 INT./EXT. CLEMENTINE'S CAR - NIGHT 93
scenes.summary: Clementine drives crying and holding Patrick's hand, asking what's wrong with her while Patrick lists her wonderful qualities.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 94
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak arrives at Joel's apartment surprised to see Mary there, she claims she came to learn, and he quickly gets the signal back on the computer.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. KITCHEN - DAY 95
scenes.summary: Joel's mother bathes him in the sink while Clementine sits in the water with him laughing, and Joel shows her his Huckleberry Hound doll.

scenes.heading: 96 INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 96
scenes.summary: Joel sits with Clementine in his parked car outside a drive-in theater drinking wine.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 97
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak announces they're back in the system, Mary praises his skill like a surgeon, and Stan suggests Howard get some sleep.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 98
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine laugh giving voice to drive-in movie characters, she starts to fade, Joel remembers they'll find her here, and she disappears.

scenes.heading: 98A INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 98A
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak watches a blip disappear from the screen and confirms he got it.

scenes.heading: 98B INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 98B
scenes.summary: Joel desperately hugs the air where Clementine was, calls her Tangerine, and she reappears in his arms as if willed back into existence.

scenes.heading: 98C INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 98C
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak and Stan watch the blip reappear on the screen and Mierzwiak fiddles with controls, finding it odd.

scenes.heading: 98D INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 98D
scenes.summary: Joel pushes open the car door, pulls Clementine out, and they run off together while Joel maintains a tight grip on her; the car disappears and the sky transforms into Joel's apartment ceiling where Howard, Stan, and Mary hover over him with his eyes open, prompting Mierzwiak to order a hypodermic injection.

scenes.heading: 98E INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 98E
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak and Stan watch a trail of light on the monitor as Mierzwiak erases its wake, with Stan noting Joel is in memories already erased, but Mierzwiak confirms they're back on track.

scenes.heading: 98F EXT. STREET - NIGHT 98F
scenes.summary: Joel drags Clementine through decayed New York streets, sees a silhouette of himself hauling garbage bags to Lacuna, and repeats the word humiliation while Clementine tells him to think.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 99
scenes.summary: Stan reports to Mierzwiak at the door with Mary that Joel and Clementine have disappeared again, and Mary apologizes to Howard for his exhaustion as he heads back to the equipment.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. BLACK VOID - NIGHT 100
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine crouch in murky blackness while Joel whispers the word humiliation under his breath.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT 101
scenes.summary: Junior-high-sized Joel masturbates in bed with a flashlight on an increasingly pornographic comic book while a faded Clementine watches, and his mother walks in and sees what's happening before retreating.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 102
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak confirms they got Joel back on track and tells Stan he'll have to get through the procedure manually because they're running late.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. BEACH - DAY 103
scenes.summary: Clementine points at a house on the cold beach and runs toward it laughing, but the scene decays, the house disappears, and Joel grabs her arm and yanks.

scenes.heading: 104 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 104
scenes.summary: Clementine holds a pillow over Joel's face while they both laugh, he goes limp pretending to be dead, she panics and shakes him, then he starts laughing and she calls him terrible.

scenes.heading: 105 EXT. JOEL'S CHILDHOOD SUBURBAN STREET - DAY 105
scenes.summary: Five-year-old Joel, goaded by other boys, miserably smashes a dead bird repeatedly with a hammer while little-girl Clementine watches with the other kids.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 106
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak locates and eradicates a small area of light in the brain imaging while Mary sits on the bed and tells him she likes watching him work; Stan leaves for a smoke, and Mary asks Howard if he likes quotes, then recites a Nietzsche quote about blessed memory erasure before they kiss.

scenes.heading: 107 INT./EXT. THE VAN/JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY 107
scenes.summary: Stan smokes in the van and watches through Joel's bedroom window as Mierzwiak and Mary talk seriously, then kiss and grope each other before falling onto the bed alongside unconscious Joel; a middle-aged woman arrives and checks the building address as Stan recognizes her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mierzwiak's wife arrives and discovers his affair with Mary, creating a major crisis that will derail the procedure and expose past secrets.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - DAY 108
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine walking hand-in-hand look up simultaneously.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 109 *
scenes.summary: Mary looks confusedly at Howard, realizes who's at the door, and exclaims in horror as Mierzwiak puts on his coat and rushes out.

scenes.heading: 110 EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 110 *
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak hurries to Hollis at her car and claims it's a one-time mistake while Mary heroically takes the blame, saying she forced herself on him, but Hollis reveals Mary had the procedure done before and doesn't remember their previous affair.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Mary had her memory of an affair with Mierzwiak erased before shatters her understanding of her own past and exposes the ethical corruption at the heart of Lacuna.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. CHARLES RIVER - NIGHT 111
scenes.summary: Clementine and Patrick lie on the frozen Charles River looking at the night sky; Patrick says he's never been happier, but Clementine sobs, says she wants to go home, then hurries toward shore, slips on ice, and runs away.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - NIGHT 113
scenes.summary: Mierzwiak and Stan work in deathly silence as Mierzwiak locates a light hidden deep in Joel's brain map and targets it.

scenes.heading: 114 EXT. ROWBOAT/INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - DAY 114
scenes.summary: Joel draws a portrait of skeleton-costumed Clementine in his apartment while the reverse angle shows his drunk, sullen father fishing in a rowboat and warning Joel not to waste his life like he did.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. THEATER - NIGHT 115
scenes.summary: Clementine leads Joel into a crowd outside a Broadway theater, adopts a mock-sophisticated tone about the play, and leads him back inside when the lights flash.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. THEATER - NIGHT 117
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine walk past the usher discussing what Clementine wore when they first met; Joel remembers her red hair and a black dress with buttons, but Clementine corrects him that he was with her when she bought that dress later.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. DRESS SHOP - DAY 118
scenes.summary: The already-erased dress shop scene shows only a decayed husk with a vague Joel watching a vague Clementine model a black dress.

scenes.heading: 119 INT. THEATER - NIGHT 119
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine search for unoccupied theater seats while discussing what black outfit she wore; Clementine now wears a generic black dress, they confirm they talked about Naomi, and Joel says he's sure now and they kiss.

scenes.heading: 120 INT./EXT. JOEL'S CAR - NIGHT 120
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine pull up to her house and she invites him up in a Mae West voice, but Joel says it's very late.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 121
scenes.summary: Joel and Clementine have awkward shy sex for their first time as Joel notes this was their first time and watches the fading scene as Clementine disappears.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. LACUNA RECEPTION AREA - NIGHT 122
scenes.summary: Mary enters the dark Lacuna reception area, flips on lights, frantically searches file folders and drops them on the floor, then exits into the inner office area.

scenes.heading: 122A INT. MIERZWIAK'S OFFICE - NIGHT 122A
scenes.summary: Mary rifles through Mierzwiak's office, desk, and closet until she finds a file with her name on it and plays the tape, hearing herself discuss liking Howard at the job interview and falling in love with him.

scenes.heading: 124 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 124 *
scenes.summary: Joel sits in his fading apartment reflecting in voiceover that he wondered if leaving Naomi was a terrible mistake, told himself they weren't happy and it was unfair to stay for safety, thought about the spark with Clementine but also what Naomi and he had built together.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. ROOM - DAY 125 *
scenes.summary: An elderly man sits as Joel's voiceover describes projecting himself to the end of his life and imagining looking back with a tremendous hole of regret in his heart.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 126 *
scenes.summary: Joel sits on the couch with a ghostly image of Naomi curled up on the other end as he says he didn't pick up the phone to call her, and the scene dissolves.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. BORDER'S BOOKSTORE - NIGHT 127 *
scenes.summary: Joel tells Clementine in a fogging-over bookstore scene that he told Naomi he needs to end it, and Clementine asks if that's what he wants before the scene fades.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. PARK - DAY 128
scenes.summary: Joel tells Naomi in the park that maybe they're not happy together, she challenges his use of 'we' when he means 'you,' he tries to explain they're both unhappy, and she accuses him of meeting someone else.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. BARNES AND NOBLE BOOKSTORE - NIGHT 129
scenes.summary: Joel enters Barnes and Noble looking for Clementine, finds her in Philosophy, apologizes for running away, asks to take her out, but she says he's married and she's not interested in him; he persists and she reluctantly agrees to meet him at the bookstore the next night.

scenes.heading: 133 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - DAY 133
scenes.summary: Joel puts on a sweater while Naomi writes at the dining table in their fading apartment; he asks if she minds him going out, she says she has to finish her chapter, he kisses her head, and she tells him to have fun and say hi to Rob and Carrie.

scenes.heading: 136 EXT. BEACH PARKING LOT - DAY 136
scenes.summary: Rob, Carrie, and Joel emerge from their car in an otherwise empty beach parking lot.

scenes.heading: 137 EXT. BEACH - DAY 137
scenes.summary: Joel watches his shoes in the sand as he trudges along while Carrie asks Rob if they're going the right way.

scenes.heading: 138 EXT. BEACH - DAY 138
scenes.summary: Joel, Rob, and Carrie step out of the brush and see a bonfire down the beach with people and music.

scenes.heading: 139 EXT. BEACH - DAY 139
scenes.summary: Joel sits on a log eating while watching couples at the bonfire and spots Clementine in her orange sweatshirt looking out at the ocean; he reflects on being drawn to her back, then she sits next to him and they begin talking about poetry, her green hair, and whether he's married.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel and Clementine meet for the first time at the beach party, the foundational moment of their entire relationship that is now being erased.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. BEACH (NEAR BEACH HOUSE) - DUSK 140
scenes.summary: Clementine tries doors on beach houses and asks Joel about his girlfriend Naomi while searching for an unlocked house.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. BEACH HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 141
scenes.summary: Joel enters the darkened beach house and Clementine closes the door behind him; she finds a flashlight, searches for candles and alcohol, declares it their house for the night as David and Ruth Laskin, and tells Joel to make drinks while she goes to slip into something more Ruth.

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. BEACH - NIGHT 142
scenes.summary: Joel finds himself hurrying back to the bonfire as the disintegrating scene dries up and he stands on a faded beach with the frozen bonfire in the distance like a photograph.

scenes.heading: 143 INT./EXT. ROB AND CARRIE'S CAR - NIGHT 143
scenes.summary: Joel sits glumly in Rob and Carrie's back seat as Carrie asks if he had fun; he studies the faded husks of memories piled like refuse outside the car window, sees dried-out versions of previous Clementine interactions playing in loops, watches his ride home decay, and everything crumbles into dust and goes black.
scenes.turningPointNote: All of Joel's memories of Clementine are completely erased as everything crumbles into dust and goes black, marking the completion of the procedure.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - EARLY MORNING 144
scenes.summary: Howard watches the monitor as the last specks of light fade to darkness; he turns to Stan who stares out at the dawn, says okay, and Stan wordlessly begins cleanup while Howard calls home and hangs up when the answering machine picks up.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. MIERZWIAK'S OFFICE AREA - EARLY MORNING 145
scenes.summary: Mary sits in the corner listening to the tape and crying as she hears herself on the recording talk about the abortion Howard said she had to have and his promise that the procedure would make her the happy Mary she once was.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - EARLY MORNING 146
scenes.summary: Stan and Howard load the last equipment into the van, look at each other, and Howard thanks Stan and says they'll talk before Stan drives off without responding.

scenes.heading: 147 INT./EXT. CLEMENTINE'S CAR - EARLY MORNING 147
scenes.summary: Patrick tries to break the silence with depressed Clementine as they drive home from Boston, suggesting coffee and commenting on the beautiful river, but she remains silent.

scenes.heading: 148 EXT. CITY STREET - EARLY MORNING 148
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 149 INT. JOEL'S BEDROOM - MORNING 149
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 149A EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - MORNING 149A
scenes.summary: Joel discovers a dent in his car but doesn't know how it got there.

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. COMMUTER TRAIN STATION - MORNING 150
scenes.summary: Joel waits on a crowded platform and boards a packed commuter train.

scenes.heading: 151 INT./EXT. MARY'S CAR - MORNING 151 *
scenes.summary: Mary cries while listening to a tape in her car, which is filled with Lacuna files in the backseat.

scenes.heading: 152 INT. JOEL'S OFFICE - MORNING 152 *
scenes.summary: Joel nervously calls Naomi from his office and arranges to take her to dinner that night.

scenes.heading: 153 INT. MARY'S APARTMENT - MORNING 153 *
scenes.summary: Mary tells Mierzwiak she doesn't like what he does to people and refuses to return the files, saying the memories are not his.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mary refuses to return the Lacuna files to Mierzwiak, taking a stand against the memory erasure practice

scenes.heading: 154 EXT. CITY STREET - NIGHT 154
scenes.summary: Joel and Naomi walk together at night; she reveals she's been seeing someone, and Joel tries to show enthusiasm.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 156
scenes.summary: Clementine cries in bed while Patrick frantically flips through Joel's journal looking for tips.

scenes.heading: 157 EXT. COMMUTER TRAIN STATION - MORNING 157
scenes.summary: Joel breaks from the crowded platform, rushes across the overpass, and boards an almost empty train on the opposite side.

scenes.heading: 158 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 158
scenes.summary: Joel says goodbye to Clementine at her apartment and she asks him to call tonight to test the phone lines.

scenes.heading: 159 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 159
scenes.summary: Joel calls Naomi and tells her he can't fall back into their familiar pattern, effectively ending their rekindled relationship.

scenes.heading: 161 EXT. CHARLES RIVER - NIGHT 161
scenes.summary: Clementine leads Joel onto the frozen Charles River; she falls and lies on her back looking at stars, and Joel eventually joins her despite his fear.

scenes.heading: 162 INT. JOEL'S CAR - MORNING 162
scenes.summary: Joel wakes Clementine in his car outside her house; she asks to come to his place to sleep and goes inside to get her toothbrush.

scenes.heading: 163 INT. CLEMENTINE'S APARTMENT - MORNING 163
scenes.summary: Clementine packs an overnight bag while Patrick's worried voice message plays in the background.

scenes.heading: 163A INT. JOEL'S CAR - MORNING 163A
scenes.summary: Clementine gets in Joel's car with her overnight bag and reads a note revealing she hired a company to erase part of her memory; Joel listens to the accompanying tape of his own voice describing their relationship.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joel and Clementine both discover they had each other erased from their memories, a devastating revelation that recontextualizes their entire reconnection

scenes.heading: 163B INT. CLEMENTINE'S CAR - MORNING 163B
scenes.summary: Clementine drives slowly down Joel's street, finds his car, and parks behind it.

scenes.heading: 163C EXT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - MORNING 163C
scenes.summary: Clementine approaches Joel's apartment building and Frank the neighbor holds the door open for her, calling her by name though she doesn't recognize him.

scenes.heading: 164A INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT BUILDING - MORNING 164A
scenes.summary: Clementine finds Joel's apartment door ajar, hears his voice inside, and enters.

scenes.heading: 165 INT. JOEL'S APARTMENT - DAY 165
scenes.summary: Clementine finds Joel listening to a tape of his own voice criticizing her; he shows her a drawing he made of her in a skeleton costume.

scenes.heading: 165A INT. KITCHEN - DAY 165A
scenes.summary: Joel retrieves an almost empty bottle of scotch from the kitchen and pours two glasses.

scenes.heading: 166 INT. JOEL'S STUDY - DAY 166
scenes.summary: Joel gives Clementine whiskey while his taped voice accuses her of using sex to get people to like her; she denies it and he says he wouldn't have thought so.

scenes.heading: 168 INT. APARTMENT HALLWAY - DAY 168 *
scenes.summary: Clementine walks down the hall to leave; Joel stops her, awkwardly asks about her bruise, and when she continues leaving he calls out that he came up with another hair color for her.
